A shadow sitting low in the back seat.  Her heart races. She turns to look,  but there's nothing there.  Just empty seats. She locks the doors,  drives off, trying to shake the feeling.  But as she stops at the next red light,  a truck pulls up fast behind her,  flashing its high beams, blaring the horn.  She panics, speeds away.  The truck follows, lights flashing,  horn screaming. When she finally pulls over,  the truck driver jumps out,  shouting at her not to move,  telling her there's someone in her backseat.  She looks, and there he is.  A man, eyes wide,  holding a knife.  They say the man in the backseat waits for distracted drivers,  lurking in parking lots, flipping inside unnoticed,  waiting for the perfect moment to strike.  So next time you get in your car,  check the back seat, or you might not make it home.

transcript: Cologne Cathedral is a Gothic masterpiece that took 632 years to build,  located in Germany. It's the third tallest church in the world  and the tallest twin spired church in the world,  reaching a height of 157 meters.  Use of mattresses helps to maintain the building  and allows the number of windows to be increased for more light.  The rib vaults  with pointed arches allows the walls and pillars to be thinner,  distributing the forces in such a way that the ceiling supports itself.  All these techniques made it possible to build cathedrals  that were much taller than ever before.  Upon its completion in 1880,  Cologne Cathedral  reigned as the world's tallest building for four years  until the Washington Monument was completed.  Inside, it contains the sacred relic of the biblical three kings,  which has made it a place of pilgrimage  since the beginning of its construction.  Despite being bombed during World War 2,  Cologne Cathedral survived  and stands as a testament to human ingenuity,  perseverance and faith, dominating the city skyline.

So yesterday I made a video about god and it got a lot of traction.  Unfortunately, people aren't addressing my main claim in the video,  which is my main contention with Christianity,  which is how can we have free will with an all knowing god?  Please understand, I am not saying that foreknowledge means causation,  but I am saying that foreknowledge necessitates a fixed future.  Let me break it down for you.  Omniscience means knowing everything,  and that knowledge is also infallible.  God cannot be wrong.  If god knows that you'll have pizza at noon tomorrow for lunch,  that is a guaranteed truth,  not just a guess or a prediction.  So if something is guaranteed to happen,  is there any way that it won't?  No, because then that knowledge would have been wrong.  God cannot be wrong.  So you cannot choose anything other than that guaranteed outcome  without breaking God's omniscience.  I know you probably already have a problem with this,  but stick with me. Let's say there's future A and future B.  Future A is you eat pizza.  Future B is you eat salad.  If god knows that you'll eat pizza,  you cannot eat salad. Not because you won't eat the salad,  but because you care. Otherwise God's knowledge becomes false.  Do we feel like we make choices?  Absolutely. So you could feel internally like,  I could eat pizza or I could eat a salad.  But you never could have done anything  other than what was already known  by God to happen.  Your sense of choice is internal,  but the outcome has already been externally settled.  Lots of people said, well,  the future is what it is because of a future choice I make.  But you can't explain the future with part of the future.  You're explaining the outcome with part of that outcome.  When you say that my fixed future happens because of my future choice,  but my future choice is part of my fixed future,  then you're just saying that it causes itself,  which is circular reasoning.  If your entire life has already been seen  and the ending has already been read,  then that story is already written.  If that story is already written,  you can't write it. You simply turn to the pages.
